content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 24, 1990


George Luna
Planning Commissioner
City of Atascadero
10600 San Marcos
Atascadero, CA 93422

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-430

Dear Commissioner Luna:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice regarding your responsibilities as a planning
commissioner for the City of Atascadero under Section 84308 of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

This letter confirms that your letter dated June 20, 1990 ac-
curately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on June
19, 1990. In our telephone conversation you asked if Section
84308 of the Act precluded you from speaking against a tentative
tract map for a parcel in your jurisdiction when the map was
considered by the city council.

As I explained in our telephone conversation, Section 84308
only precludes disqualification from any decision in a proceeding
involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use pending
before the agency,2/ if the officer has received a contribution in
an amount of $250 or more within the preceding 12 months from a
party or his or her agent, or from any participant who has a
financial interest in the decision. (Section 84308(c).)


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Section 84308 applies only to officers of appointed boards or
commissions with respect to proceedings involving licenses,
permits, or other entitlements for use. (Regulation 18438.1;
Coleman Advice Letter, No. A-89-265, copies enclosed.)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No.  A-90-430
Page 2


     Regulation 18438.2 defines proceedings regulated by Section
84308 as follows:

     (a)  For purposes of Government Code Section
84308, a "proceeding involving a license, permit or
other entitlement for use" includes any proceeding
to grant, deny, revoke, restrict, or modify a
license, permit or other entitlement for use.

     (b)  A proceeding involving a license, permit
or other entitlement for use is "pending before" an
agency:

          (1)  When the application has been filed,
the proceeding has been commenced, or the
issue has been otherwise submitted to the
jurisdiction of an agency for its
determination or other action;

          (2)  It is the type of proceeding where
the officers of the agency are required by law
to make a decision, or the matter has been
otherwise submitted to the officers of the
agency for their decision; and,

          (3)  The decision of the officer or
officers with respect to the proceeding will
not be purely ministerial.

     According to your facts, the planning commission proceeding
has been completed.  You did not participate in the planning
commission proceeding because of a $300 contribution received from
a participant3/ in that proceeding for your campaign for city
council.  The city council is now considering the issue.  Section
84308 does not prevent you from acting in your private capacity
before the city council.4/


_____________
3/     Section 84308(a)(2). defines "participant" to be any person who
is not a party but who actively supports or opposes a particular
decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use and who has a financial interest in the
decision.

4/     This conclusion is consistent with the rule applied to
conflict-of-interest situations in Regulation 18700.1 (copy
enclosed) which precludes participation only before the official's
agency or an agency under the budgetary control of the official's
agency. (Brown Advice Letter, No. A-84-131; Harron Advice Letter,
No. A-83-184, copies enclosed.)

Our File No.  A-90-430
Page 3


If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:dg

sections.question: Does Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act preclude you from speaking against a tentative tract map for a parcel in your jurisdiction when the map is considered by the city council?
sections.conclusion: Section 84308 does not prevent you from acting in your private capacity before the city council. The disqualification requirement under Section 84308 applies only to proceedings before the officer's own agency (the planning commission), not to subsequent proceedings before a different agency (the city council).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a planning commissioner who received a $300 campaign contribution from a participant in a planning commission proceeding can speak against that matter when it is subsequently considered by the city council.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that Section 84308's disqualification requirement does not apply to the city council proceeding because the conflict-of-interest rules only preclude participation before the official's own agency, not different agencies.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Advice letter confirming prior telephone advice; question and conclusion clearly identifiable from letter content; minor OCR artifacts present but do not affect meaning extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act preclude you from speaking against a tentative tract map for a parcel in your jurisdiction when the map is considered by the city council?

CONCLUSION: Section 84308 does not prevent you from acting in your private capacity before the city council. The disqualification requirement under Section 84308 applies only to proceedings before the officer's own agency (the planning commission), not to subsequent proceedings before a different agency (the city council).

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding whether a planning commissioner must disqualify from speaking on a matter before the city council due to a $300 campaign contribution received from a participant in the prior planning commission proceeding on the same matter.
